---
title: "About"
description: "Professional profile of Miguel Cosenza Contreras."
---

Miguel Cosenza Contreras is a proteomics data scientist based in Freiburg im Breisgau, Germany. Trained as a biologist, he works at the interface of experimental design and data analysis, with a focus on mass-spectrometry-based proteomics in protein interaction research, cancer, cardiac disease, and infection biology.

Miguel has over six years of experience in clinical and experimental research. His work involves developing and applying statistical and computational methods for the integrative analysis of proteomics, terminomics, lipidomics, and proteogenomics data.

Since April 2024, Miguel has worked as a postdoctoral researcher / bioinformatician in the Laboratory of Protease Degradomics and SFB1381 - Dynamic Organization of Cellular Protein Machineries at the Faculty of Biology, University of Freiburg, with Prof. Dr. Pitter Huesgen. His current work focuses on building and maintaining bioinformatic pipelines for studying protein interactions and proteolytic activity, including complexome profiling, co-immunoprecipitation assays, blue-native gel mass spectrometry experiments, and shotgun mass-spectrometry-based proteomics data analysis.

From 2019 to 2024, he was a doctoral researcher in the Laboratory of Translational Proteomics at the Institute of Surgical Pathology, University Medical Center Freiburg, supervised by Prof. Dr. Oliver Schilling. His doctoral work focused on proteomics data science and multi-omics data integration for exploratory insight into disease mechanisms.

A recurring theme across his work is reproducibility: using R, Quarto, transparent project structure, and reusable analysis code to make complex proteomics workflows easier to inspect, adapt, and extend.

## Research Themes

::: {.content-grid}
::: {.info-card}
### Protein Complexes
Bioinformatic pipelines for studying protein-protein interactions from shotgun proteomics, complexome profiling, co-immunoprecipitation, and blue-native gel mass spectrometry experiments.
:::

::: {.info-card}
### Proteolytic Processing
Computational analysis of protein termini, cleavage events, proteolytic activity, and degradomics experiments.
:::

::: {.info-card}
### Multi-omics Data Science
Statistical and computational integration of proteomics with terminomics, lipidomics, proteogenomics, and clinical variables.
:::

::: {.info-card}
### Reproducible Analysis
Human-readable R, Bioconductor, Quarto, Markdown, and GitHub workflows for mass-spectrometry data analysis.
:::

::: {.info-card}
### Clinical Proteomics
Quantitative proteomics and biomarker-oriented analysis in translational disease studies.
:::
:::
